To: Board of Directors, Harbor Jade Holdings Limited (“HJH”)
From: Legal & Compliance (Ms. Serene Kwan), contact: serene.kwan@harborjade.test
Date: 30 May 2026
Subject: Inside Information Controls — Project Coral and related cyber/ESG matters under the SFO/PDPO

HJH is currently in possession of inside information within the meaning of Part XIVA of the SFO and must manage disclosure and confidentiality under the Listing Rules and internal Information Barrier Policy.
Project Coral: HJH has signed a non-binding term sheet to acquire 60% of Aetheryn Vault (HK) Limited (a virtual asset custody applicant), with HKD 92m upfront and a 30% earn-out contingent on licence grant; this has not been announced and is reasonably expected to be price-sensitive.
Cyber: On 28 May a malware intrusion at subsidiary Pearl Circuit Retail was contained; preliminary forensics indicate limited access to a sandbox email registry (hashed addresses only), no payment data, and estimated remediation costs of HKD 4–6m; this assessment is internal and not yet disclosed.
ESG/climate: A draft external assurance memo indicates our FY25 Scope 2 baseline may be understated by c.14%, affecting a sustainability-linked loan step-up trigger and covenant headroom; lender discussions are ongoing and no public update has been released.
Until announcement, a dealing blackout applies from 1 June to interim results publication; the insider list for Project Coral is to include Ms. Serene Kwan, Mr. Leon Cho (CFO), and Dr. Mei Lau (Head of Digital Assets), with access logged in the ST Insider Register.
Information barriers: Research should remain out-of-the-wall; any wall-crossing requires Legal clearance; calendar invites titled “Research Teach-in” during live diligence should be avoided to mitigate appearance of selective disclosure.
Investor interactions: The 3 June meeting with North Reef Family Office must use the approved “public-only” deck; no forward-looking DPT revenue, cyber loss estimates, or ESG KPI sensitivities are to be discussed.
Educational note: The “Crypto and Climate Controls” brochure and cyber wallet policy used by Marketing are training materials only and contain no issuer-specific numbers, client names, or live wallet references.
Employment and clean team: Seconded analysts from Velvet Peak Capital SPC will execute enhanced NDAs, and a clean team (code name “Coral-CT”) will handle VA diligence with masked client sets; correspondence via cleanteam@harborjade.test.
PDPO: If any personal data risk is confirmed, notify Legal within 2 hours for assessment of breach notification steps and vendor contractual remedies; do not circulate screenshots or raw logs outside the Coral distribution.
Any media, supplier, or buy-side queries should be routed to Ms. Kwan only; tipping risk is high and all staff remain subject to the Communications Code and Dealing Restrictions Policy.
